questions.question: The indigenous CAR‑T therapy has shown maximum efficacy against B‑ALL and which other blood cancer?
Which of the following diseases is caused by protozoa?
Which medical term describes an eating disorder in which energy expenditure is restricted below requirements, leading to significant weight loss?
Which of the following is classified as micronutrients?
Which of the following organs represents the female reproductive part of a flower in angiosperms?
In which chronic disease do the inner walls of the lungs' air sacs, called alveoli, become damaged, causing them to eventually burst?
Which novel macrolide antibiotic, highlighted in a December 2024 PIB factsheet, is being positioned as India’s first indigenous drug against community‑acquired pneumonia?
When did the Hershey-Chase experiment provide solid evidence that DNA, not proteins, is the genetic material?
Amylopectin is a branched-chain polysaccharide composed of glucose units linked primarily by ________.
Which disease is caused by the accumulation of lipids in brain cells due to the deficiency or inactivation of lipid digesting enzymes?
Which of the following options best describes the purpose of the Red Data Book?
questions.option_1: A list of protected forest areas
questions.option_2: A record of endangered plant and animal species
questions.option_3: A compilation of forest conservation laws

questions.question: In May 2025, an Indian medical team contributed to an international study demonstrating that AI can predict life-threatening heart arrhythmias up to two weeks in advance. Which condition was the AI model primarily designed to predict?
Which is the deadliest species of Plasmodium that causes malaria in humans?
What is the name of the genome-edited rice variety developed to improve yield and productivity in May 2025?
questions.option_1: DRR Dhan 100 (Kamala) and Pusa DST Rice 1
questions.option_3: Malaviya Manila Sinchit Dhan-1 and Pusa Basmati 1121

questions.question: Which of the following is a water pollutant?
Which of the following parts of the plant is primarily responsible for sexual reproduction in angiosperms?
Which biomolecules are responsible for catalysing biochemical reactions?
What are endemic species?
questions.option_1: Species found only in a particular area and nowhere else
questions.option_2: Exotic animals brought from other countries

questions.question: What is the name of the medical instrument used to listen to internal body sounds, especially from the heart and lungs?
The Human Genome Project, launched in 1990, had the goal of determining the sequence of how many billion base pairs that make up human DNA?
Which of the following correctly represents the function of dietary fibre in the human diet?
Which of the following pigments is present in vegetables like spinach, giving it the characteristic green colour?
Consider the following vitamins.1. Vitamin A2. Vitamin B63. Vitamin C4. Vitamin DWhich of them is/are fat-soluble?
The Indian Institute of Spices Research (IISR), Kozhikode, has developed a light-coloured turmeric variety named:
In humans, which blood vessels carry oxygen-rich blood away from the heart to all the body parts?
